reviews.text: This film blew me away. The performances were outstanding and the story kept me hooked from start to finish.
Absolutely terrible. The plot made no sense and the acting was wooden throughout.
One of the best movies I've seen in years. Beautifully shot, brilliantly acted.
A complete waste of time. Predictable story, flat characters, and a dull ending.
I laughed, I cried, I was completely absorbed. A masterpiece of modern cinema.
Boring from the first scene to the last. I nearly fell asleep twice.
The director brought something truly special to the screen. Highly recommended.
Poorly written dialogue and a story that goes nowhere. Disappointing.
A stunning visual experience backed by a deeply moving story.
The worst film I've seen this year. Cheap, lazy, and insulting to the audience.
Every scene felt purposeful. The editing was sharp and the score was perfect.
Over-hyped and under-delivered. The trailer was better than the actual film.
Genuinely one of the most gripping thrillers in recent memory.
Tried too hard to be clever and ended up being confusing and irritating.
Warm, funny, and surprisingly emotional. A real crowd-pleaser.
The pacing was atrocious. Every scene dragged on far too long.
A bold, ambitious film that delivers on every promise it makes.
Shallow characters and a recycled plot. Nothing here we haven't seen before.
The lead actor carries the entire film with a magnetic performance.
Incoherent mess from start to finish. I couldn't follow what was happening.
A refreshingly original story told with confidence and style.
Felt like a student film. Amateurish direction and poor production values.
Left me thinking for days. Rare that a movie achieves that.
The CGI was embarrassingly bad and distracted from everything else.
Smart, funny, and heartfelt. Everything you could want from a film.
Ridiculous plot holes and a climax that made absolutely no sense.
The chemistry between the leads was electric. A joy to watch.
Tonally inconsistent and never quite decided what kind of film it wanted to be.
An instant classic. I will be watching this again many times.
Completely forgettable. Within an hour of leaving the cinema I'd forgotten the plot.
Powerful performances and a script that respects the audience's intelligence.
The humour fell completely flat. Not a single scene made me smile.
A tight, well-crafted thriller that never lets up. Excellent.
Painfully slow and self-indulgent. The director clearly needed an editor.
The world-building was incredible. I was totally immersed.
A cynical cash-grab with no artistic merit whatsoever.
Moved me deeply. One of those rare films that actually says something meaningful.
Worst screenplay of the year. Every line of dialogue felt forced and unnatural.
Funny, touching and beautifully made. A real gem.
The villain was cartoonish and the hero utterly unlikeable.
A triumph of storytelling. Confident, compelling and unforgettable.
Relied entirely on shock value with nothing of substance underneath.
The supporting cast stole every scene they were in. What a talented ensemble.
Dreadfully dull. The kind of film that makes you resent the medium.
A genuinely surprising and rewarding watch. I didn't expect to love it this much.
All style and no substance. Pretty to look at but completely hollow.
The script crackled with wit and the performances matched it perfectly.
An embarrassing attempt at profundity that comes across as pretentious nonsense.
Gripping, intelligent and emotionally resonant. Cinema at its best.
A tedious experience. Felt like it was twice as long as it actually was.
The cinematography was breathtaking and the story matched it in beauty.
Poorly directed, poorly acted, and poorly written. A triple failure.
I was on the edge of my seat for the entire runtime. Absolutely gripping.
A soulless blockbuster that prioritises spectacle over story at every turn.
Rare honesty and emotional depth. This film truly moved me.
The second act completely fell apart and the film never recovered.
Witty, warm and wonderfully performed. A delight from beginning to end.
Lazy writing saved by nothing. Even the action sequences were dull.
A film with genuine soul. You can feel the passion behind every frame.
The romance felt forced and unconvincing. Zero chemistry between the leads.
Absolutely gripping. I held my breath through the final twenty minutes.
An interminable slog through clichés we've seen a thousand times before.
Every performance was pitch-perfect. A rare ensemble piece that really works.
Offensive in its stupidity. Treats the audience like complete idiots.
A lovingly crafted film full of small, perfect moments.
The twist ending retroactively ruined the entire film for me.
Exhilarating and emotionally satisfying. Left the cinema feeling uplifted.
Muddled themes and a directionless plot. What was this film even about?
One of those rare films that gets better every time you watch it.
Utterly joyless. A dark film that mistakes grimness for depth.
The dialogue was sharp, funny and completely believable. Outstanding writing.
A franchise entry that adds nothing new and retreads the same tired ground.
Visually stunning and emotionally devastating. A true cinematic achievement.
The exposition was clumsy and the world felt underbuilt and unconvincing.
Tender and truthful. A small film with an enormous heart.
Yet another disappointing sequel that tarnishes the original.
A masterclass in restraint and atmosphere. Deeply unsettling in the best way.
Formulaic to the point of self-parody. Every beat was predictable.
The kind of film that reminds you why you fell in love with cinema.
Tries to balance comedy and drama but fails spectacularly at both.
A beautifully observed character study. The lead performance is extraordinary.
Bloated runtime, repetitive action and no meaningful character development.
Quietly devastating. I was unexpectedly moved to tears.
A film that mistakes loud for impactful. Exhausting from start to finish.
Perfectly paced with a story that genuinely earned its emotional climax.
The script seemed written by someone who has never met an actual human being.
Fresh, inventive and full of ideas. A real breath of fresh air.
The message was obvious and heavy-handed. No subtlety whatsoever.
Completely absorbing. Two hours that felt like twenty minutes.
Grotesquely violent with no purpose beyond shock. Deeply unpleasant.
A sensitive and intelligent adaptation that honours the source material.
Painfully unfunny comedy that mistakes cruelty for wit.
The kind of ambitious filmmaking we rarely see any more. Bravo.
A dreary, po-faced affair with nothing interesting to say.
Infectiously joyful. Walked out with a massive smile on my face.
A total mess. Clearly recut many times and it shows in every scene.
Rich, layered storytelling that rewards patient and attentive viewers.
Cheaply made, lazily written and utterly forgettable in every way.
